question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a numeric string '0' via the block parameter so that BlockType.of('0') creates a new BlockType instance with number=0 that is NOT reference-equal (==) to BlockType.EARLIEST, causing findByBlockType() to call findByIndex(0) instead of findEarliest(), potentially returning a different record file than the true genesis block and misrepresenting the earliest transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reference equality check (block == BlockType.LATEST) in RecordFileServiceImpl by constructing a BlockType via BlockType.of() with name='latest' and number=Long.MAX_VALUE that is structurally identical to LATEST but not the same reference, causing findByIndex(Long.MAX_VALUE) to be called instead of findLatest(), returning empty and disrupting the historical view of the most recent transactions?
